summary: A brief collection of LinkedIn posts and activity from Andrej Karpathy, primarily focused on his hiring philosophy for the Tesla AI team and the core approach to autonomous driving.

principles.statement: Strong software engineering is the primary requirement for Deep Learning Engineers.
principles.rationale: Deep learning knowledge can be taught on the job, but a solid foundation in software engineering is essential for building and deploying models effectively.
principles.source_quote: Strong software engineering is the primary requirement. Except for the scientist role, deep learning interest or knowledge is only a bonus (we will teach you).

principles.statement: Autonomy at scale requires advanced AI for vision and planning.
principles.rationale: To deploy autonomous systems effectively in the real world, the core approach must be grounded in advanced AI that handles both vision and planning.
principles.source_quote: We believe that an approach based on advanced AI for vision and planning, supported by efficient use of…

heuristics: Hire for strong software engineering first; teach deep learning on the job.

signature_quotes.text: Strong software engineering is the primary requirement. Except for the scientist role, deep learning interest or knowledge is only a bonus (we will teach you).
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the hiring criteria for Deep Learning Engineers at Tesla.

anti_patterns.description: Requiring deep learning expertise for all deep learning engineering roles.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It filters out exceptionally strong software engineers who possess the necessary foundational skills to build systems and can easily be taught the deep learning specifics on the job.
